ddls:
CREATE TABLE "shoe_sizes" (
  "size_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each shoe size entry */,
  "length_mm" INTEGER /* Length of the shoe in millimeters */,
  "width_mm" INTEGER /* Width of the shoe in millimeters */,
  "uk_size" REAL /* UK shoe size for men and women */,
  "eu_size" REAL /* EU shoe size for men and women */,
  "us_men_size" REAL /* US shoe size for men */,
  "us_men_narrow_size" REAL /* US shoe size for men (narrow feet) */,
  "us_men_wide_size" REAL /* US shoe size for men (wide feet) */,
  "us_women_normal_size" REAL /* US shoe size for women (normal) */,
  "us_women_narrow_size" REAL /* US shoe size for women (narrow feet) */,
  "us_women_wide_size" REAL /* US shoe size for women (wide feet) */,
  "us_women_injected_size" REAL /* US shoe size for women (injected soles) */,
  "uk_women_size" REAL /* UK shoe size for women */,
  "eu_women_size" REAL /* EU shoe size for women */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("size_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shoe_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each shoe model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the shoe model */,
  "brand" TEXT /* Brand of the shoe */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the shoe (e.g., athletic, formal) */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the shoe model */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender for which the shoe is designed (e.g., men, women, unisex) */,
  "size_chart_id" INTEGER /* ID of the size chart associated with this model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the shoe model */,
  "image_url" TEXT /* URL to an image of the shoe model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inventory" (
  "inventory_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inventory record */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shoe model */,
  "size_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shoe size */,
  "quantity_available" INTEGER /* Quantity available for this size and model */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the inventory location (e.g., store, warehouse) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last inventory update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("inventory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_size_id FOREIGN KEY ("size_id") REFERENCES shoe_sizes ("size_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES shoe_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* Customer's first name */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Customer's last name */,
  "email" TEXT /* Customer's email address */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Customer's phone number */,
  "address" TEXT /* Customer's address */,
  "preferred_size_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer's preferred shoe size */,
  "preferred_brand" TEXT /* Customer's preferred shoe brand */,
  "preferred_gender" TEXT /* Customer's preferred gender for shoe models */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "orders" (
  "order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who made the order */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date when the order was placed */,
  "total_amount" REAL /* Total amount for the order */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the order (e.g., pending, shipped, delivered) */,
  "shipping_address" TEXT /* Shipping address for the order */,
  "billing_address" TEXT /* Billing address for the order */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "order_items" (
  "order_item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order item */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order to which this item belongs */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shoe model */,
  "size_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shoe size */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of this item ordered */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the item at the time of the order */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES shoe_models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_size_id FOREIGN KEY ("size_id") REFERENCES shoe_sizes ("size_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "returns" (
  "return_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each return record */,
  "order_item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order item being returned */,
  "return_date" TEXT /* Date when the item was returned */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the return */,
  "refund_amount" REAL /* Amount refunded for the return */,
  "return_status" TEXT /* Status of the return (e.g., pending, approved, rejected) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("return_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_returns_order_item_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_item_id") REFERENCES order_items ("order_item_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, staff) */,
  "email" TEXT /* User's email address */,
  "phone" TEXT /* User's phone number */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "size_charts" (
  "chart_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each size chart entry */,
  "size_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shoe size */,
  "region" TEXT /* The region the size applies to (e.g., US, EU, UK) */,
  "standard" TEXT /* The sizing standard used */,
  "chart_description" TEXT /* Description of the size chart */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("chart_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_size_charts_size_id FOREIGN KEY ("size_id") REFERENCES shoe_sizes ("size_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics record */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shoe model sold */,
  "size_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shoe size sold */,
  "sales_count" INTEGER /* Number of items sold */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the sales record */,
  "return_count" INTEGER /* Number of returns for the item */,
  "feedback_score" REAL /* Average customer feedback score for the item */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES shoe_models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_size_id FOREIGN KEY ("size_id") REFERENCES shoe_sizes ("size_id")
)
